tagline: Achiote-chipotle pork over cilantro-lime rice with a corn-poblano crunch.
signal_rank_note: Al pastor is an emerging — not dominant — signal in Williamsburg; the neighborhood's Mexican gravity is coastal (Ensenada) more than taqueria, so this plays as a familiar comfort rather than a trend headline.
novelty_check: Chipotle has no fixed dish list; this leans into the LTO al pastor protein as a composed bowl with pineapple-forward identity rather than the default chicken/steak builds.
confidence_reason: Al pastor is already an LTO SKU so execution risk is zero, but the 0.28 score means it's a modest-pull dish, not a hero.

dish_potential: Roasted cauliflower as a veg-forward protein alternative unlocks a 'Cauliflower Adobo Bowl' line — adobo-roasted florets over cilantro-lime rice with tomatillo red chili and queso blanco — plus a vegetarian taco that doesn't default to sofritas or fajita veggies.
operational_lift: Roast-and-hold cauliflower fits the existing sheet-pan/fajita-veggie prep flow with no new equipment.
comp_context: Kokomo runs charred cauliflower as a Caribbean-spiced shareable, and the format reads as 'healthy indulgence' to the Williamsburg lunch crowd.

tagline: Slow-braised lentils, lemon-herb tahini, sumac-bright crunch.
signal_rank_note: Tahini is a genuinely strong signal in Williamsburg — K'Far and Laser Wolf have made it a default flavor reference, so CAVA is playing in-pocket here, not chasing.
novelty_check: CAVA is assembly-line; this build foregrounds lentils + tahini as the headline pairing (rather than hummus or harissa-forward defaults) and uses saffron rice as a co-base for a more composed eating experience.
confidence_reason: Tahini scores 0.63 with strong Israeli-indie reinforcement locally, and every ingredient is already on the line.

dish_potential: A pesto SKU opens a 'Greens + Pesto' line — pesto-dressed power greens with grilled chicken, kalamata, tomato + cucumber — bridging CAVA's Mediterranean identity into a Northern-Med / Ligurian register and giving the menu its first basil-forward dressing.
operational_lift: Pesto is a cold-line scoop alongside existing dips/dressings — no new station, just an additional refrigerated well.
comp_context: Williamsburg Italian-leaning spots (and the broader Brooklyn deli-counter scene) treat pesto as a default green sauce; CAVA currently cedes that whole flavor to competitors.

tagline: Miso-glazed salmon, apple kimchi, crispy rice — sweet-heat-funk in one bowl.
signal_rank_note: Miso is a top-tier signal (0.80) in Williamsburg — this is squarely in-trend, not catching up.
novelty_check: Distinct from Miso Glazed Salmon (which is greens-forward with herbs) and Crispy Rice Bowl (spicy cashew + steak frame) — this combines the salmon protein with apple kimchi and a miso-sesame dressing in a wild-rice base, a build none of the 27 existing dishes assembles.
confidence_reason: Miso scores 0.80, every SKU is already on the line, and Williamsburg over-indexes on Japanese-leaning health-bowl formats.

dish_potential: Roasted or crispy plantain unlocks a Caribbean-leaning bowl — 'Jerk Chicken Plantain Bowl' with blackened chicken, plantain, corn salsa, shredded cabbage, lime cilantro jalapeño sauce — and a sweet-savory topping that's currently absent from the entire topping rail.
operational_lift: Plantain needs a roast-and-hold step similar to sweet potatoes, so it slots into existing warm-veg prep with minimal added complexity.
comp_context: Kokomo's whole identity is plantain-forward Caribbean (jerk, curry, oxtail), and Williamsburg diners already pay premium for that flavor profile — Sweetgreen has zero answer to it today.
